page: Hetzner Cloud ccx63 is a dedicated CPU virtual server instance powered by the AMD EPYC Milan processor architecture. Running on the QEMU hypervisor, this x86_64 platform features 48 dedicated vCPUs derived from 24 physical cores with multithreading enabled, operating at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The processor configuration includes a cache hierarchy of 32 KB L1d, 0.5 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 188.0 GB of system memory, providing approximately 3.91 GB of RAM per core, and includes 960 GB of local SSD storage.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong multi-core and single-core CPU performance, placing the instance in the top 25% of tested systems under stress-ng and PassMark CPU tests. The hardware demonstrates strong capabilities in cryptographic tasks, database operations, and static web serving, with Redis SET operations reaching 64.55 million operations per second and static web serving throughput measured at 41.6 GB/sec. Memory bandwidth tests show a strong write speed for large blocks and high read speeds for small cached blocks, though large block read performance falls into the average category. For language model workloads, the server achieves top-tier performance in text generation for small 135M models, while medium and large model inference speeds remain in the average to strong ranges.

From a resource density perspective, the ccx63 offers a balanced vCPU-to-RAM ratio of nearly 1:4, aligning it with general-purpose and compute-intensive requirements without the premium associated with specialized memory-optimized instances. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing capabilities, avoiding the performance degradation common in shared-core environments. However, the lack of hardware acceleration limits its utility for deep learning training, and the 960 GB local SSD storage capacity may require external storage attachments for large-scale data warehousing. This configuration is suitable for database hosting, software compilation, web serving, and moderate-scale LLM inference workloads.
description: Hetzner Cloud ccx63 is a dedicated virtual server built on the x86_64 AMD EPYC Milan architecture, utilizing the QEMU hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs across 24 physical cores, 188.0 GB of RAM, and 960 GB of local SSD storage. The instance delivers strong multi-core processing and cryptographic performance, placing in the top 25% of benchmarks such as stress-ng and PassMark. It achieves 64.55 million operations per second in Redis SET tests and 41.6 GB/sec in static web serving throughput. While memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks is average, its small-block cached read and large-block write speeds are strong. Lacking a GPU, it is not designed for heavy parallel acceleration but offers a cost-efficient, high-density CPU profile. The ccx63 is suited for database operations, software compilation, web serving, and text-generation workloads.
og_description: Hetzner Cloud ccx63 is a dedicated virtual server featuring 48 AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs, 188.0 GB of RAM, and 960 GB of SSD storage, delivering strong multi-core CPU and database performance in benchmark testing.
meta_description: Hetzner Cloud ccx63 is a dedicated x86_64 server with 48 AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs, 188.0 GB of RAM, and 960 GB SSD storage for compute and database workloads.
tagline: A dedicated multi-core processor instance with balanced memory and local SSD storage, optimized for database operations and parallel CPU workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs and 188.0 GB of system memory
960 GB of local SSD storage for high-throughput data access
Strong multi-core CPU, database, and cryptographic benchmark performance
Top-tier text generation speeds for small-scale language models
Suited for database hosting, software compilation, and web serving
